skill.name: prompt-caching
skill.description: Caching strategies for LLM prompts including Anthropic prompt caching, response caching, and CAG (Cache Augmented Generation). Use when: prompt caching, cache prompt, response cache, cag, cache augmented generation.
skill.summary: Expert guidance on implementing prompt caching strategies to reduce LLM costs by up to 90%
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a markdown documentation file with no executable code. The external_commands detections are markdown backticks used for code formatting. The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are misinterpretations of YAML frontmatter content. No actual security risks present.

content.user_title: Optimize LLM Costs with Prompt Caching
content.value_statement: Reduce LLM API costs by up to 90% through strategic prompt caching implementation including Anthropic native caching, response caching, and CAG patterns.
actual_capabilities: Implement Anthropic native prompt caching for repeated prefixes
Design response caching strategies for identical or similar queries
Optimize prompts for maximum cache hit rates
Avoid caching anti-patterns like high-temperature responses
limitations: Does not provide actual caching infrastructure code
Does not integrate with specific databases or caching systems
Temperature settings must be zero for reliable caching
Cache invalidation strategy must be designed by the user

use_cases.title: Reduce API Costs for Production Applications
use_cases.description: Implement prompt caching to dramatically reduce LLM API costs in production systems with repeated context

use_cases.title: Optimize Long-Running Conversations
use_cases.description: Use caching to maintain conversation context without incurring full context costs on each message
use_cases.target_user: Developers building chatbots and conversational AI

use_cases.title: Improve Response Latency
use_cases.description: Leverage cached responses to achieve faster response times for repeated queries

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prompt Caching Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I set up prompt caching with Claude API? Show me the basic implementation steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Anthropic prompt caching

prompt_templates.title: Response Caching Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a response caching strategy for a Q&A system that handles similar user queries. Include cache key design and invalidation logic.

prompt_templates.title: CAG Implementation Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain Cache Augmented Generation (CAG) and provide a Python implementation pattern for pre-caching documents.

prompt_templates.title: Cache Invalidation Best Practices
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best practices for cache invalidation in LLM applications? Include time-based and event-based strategies.

output_examples.input: How do I reduce my LLM API costs?
output_examples.output: You can reduce LLM API costs by implementing prompt caching. The main strategies are: 1) Use Anthropic's native prompt caching for repeated system prompts - this caches the prompt prefix so you only pay for new tokens. 2) Implement response caching for identical queries - store hash of prompt and return cached response. 3) Use Cache Augmented Generation (CAG) to pre-cache documents instead of doing RAG retrieval each time.

output_examples.input: Why is my cache not working?
output_examples.output: Common reasons prompt caching fails: 1) Using non-zero temperature - cached responses vary. 2) Changing prompt prefixes frequently - cache depends on exact prefix match. 3) Not using the cache-control header properly. 4) Exceeding the cache TTL (typically 5 minutes for Anthropic).

best_practices: Structure prompts with static prefixes that remain consistent across requests
Use zero temperature when caching responses for exact matches
Implement proper cache invalidation with time-based or event-based triggers
Monitor cache hit rates and optimize prefix structure accordingly
anti_patterns: Caching responses with high temperature settings - outputs will vary and cached data becomes useless
Caching without any invalidation strategy - stale data leads to incorrect responses
Caching everything indiscriminately - increases latency on cache misses without proportional benefit

faq.question: What is prompt caching?
faq.answer: Prompt caching is a technique that stores the computed state of a prompt prefix so it can be reused across multiple requests, reducing the number of tokens processed and lowering costs.

faq.question: How much can I save with prompt caching?
faq.answer: Users report cost reductions of 50-90% depending on how much of your prompts can be cached as stable prefixes.

faq.question: Does prompt caching work with all Claude models?
faq.answer: Prompt caching is supported by Claude models that support the cache_control parameter. Check the Anthropic API documentation for model compatibility.

faq.question: What is the difference between prompt caching and response caching?
faq.answer: Prompt caching uses the model's native ability to cache computed prefixes. Response caching is implemented by you - storing full responses for identical queries in your own storage.

faq.question: How long does the cache last?
faq.answer: Anthropic's prompt cache typically lasts 5 minutes, but this varies by API version. Response caching TTL is determined by your implementation.

faq.question: Can I cache responses with temperature greater than 0?
faq.answer: You should not cache responses with non-zero temperature because the outputs will vary, making cached data unreliable.
